title: Daily Financial Briefing
marketTone: Back-to-back US inflation misses (CPI + PPI) plus ASML's second FY26 sales raise fueled a Kospi +6.2% semi rip; China Q2 GDP missed at 4.3% while H-shares outperformed A-shares — TSMC's Jul 16 print is the next AI-demand test.

summary.text: June PPI miss (BLS, Jul 15): final demand −0.3% m/m, +5.5% y/y; final demand goods less foods and energy +0.2% m/m — second straight disinflation print after Tuesday's CPI.
June CPI miss (BLS, Jul 14): headline −0.4% m/m, 3.5% y/y; core flat m/m, 2.6% y/y — largest monthly headline drop since April 2020.
ASML (Jul 15 IR): Q2 total net sales €9.3bn, net income €2.9bn; raised FY26 total net sales guidance to €43–45bn (from €36–40bn prior).
Asia Wed: Kospi +6.24% to 7,284.41 (SK hynix +8.83%); Hang Seng +1.40% to 24,681.10; Shanghai −0.29% to 3,955.58 after China Q2 GDP 4.3% miss (NBS).

signal: Disinflation relief is confirmed in CPI and PPI and ASML re-accelerated the AI-capex narrative, but Hormuz/oil risk and Warsh's hawkish filter mean September hike odds have not collapsed — TSMC Jul 16 decides whether the semi beta extends.
watch: TSMC Q2 results and guidance (Jul 16, 14:00 Taiwan / 02:00 ET) after ASML's raise and Kospi +6% — does AI demand visibility hold through Taiwan's bellwether?
globalRegime: The Fed holds at 3.50%–3.75%. June CPI and PPI both missed consensus, pulling near-term hike odds sharply lower (CME FedWatch). Chair Warsh's congressional testimony (Jul 14–15) kept a hawkish interpretive filter — zero tolerance for elevated inflation, no 'mission accomplished' on one soft print. ASML's second FY26 guidance raise and a Kospi +6% session show the AI/semi trade re-engaging beneath IBM's prior software miss. Brent traded in an ~$85–86 range Wednesday on Hormuz supply risk (spot/trading range, not a final settle).

globalChanged.text: June PPI (BLS, Jul 15): final demand −0.3% m/m after +0.6% in May; +5.5% y/y unadjusted; final demand goods −1.4% m/m with energy −6.4% m/m; goods less foods and energy +0.2% m/m.
June CPI (BLS, Jul 14): all-items −0.4% m/m, +3.5% y/y; energy −5.7% m/m; core (ex food & energy) unchanged m/m, +2.6% y/y.
Post-CPI US close (Jul 14, Reuters tape): S&P +0.38% to 7,543.89, Nasdaq +0.90% to 26,107.01, Dow ~flat at 52,508; July Fed hold odds rose to ~83% post-CPI (CME FedWatch).
ASML Q2 (Jul 15): total net sales €9.326bn, gross margin 54.0%, net income €2.918bn; FY26 sales outlook raised to €43–45bn; planning +30% low-NA EUV capacity into 2027.
Kospi (Jul 15): +6.24% to 7,284.41 with buy-side sidecar; SK hynix +8.83% to 2,082,000 won; KRW 1,484.7 vs USD (Yonhap).
TSMC Q2 earnings conference scheduled Thu Jul 16, 14:00 Taiwan time; prior Q2 revenue guidance was US$39.0–40.2bn with GM 65.5%–67.5% (TSMC IR).

globalImplies.text: Back-to-back CPI/PPI misses ease July re-hike urgency, but Brent holding the mid-$80s and Warsh's hawkish frame keep September tightening risk alive.
ASML's capacity and FY sales raise is a primary-source confirmation of AI equipment demand — supportive for Korea memory/ASML supply chain into TSMC's print.
IBM's prior software miss remains idiosyncratic vs the ASML/Kospi tape; treat it as sector rotation inside tech, not a market-wide earnings regime flip.

globalTensions: CPI/PPI say June inflation cooled on energy; spot oil near $85–86 says July CPI may re-heat. Korea/Nasdaq catch the AI bid while onshore China equities lag a GDP miss — same global impulse, split local betas.
chinaStance: Moderately loose via quantity, cautious on price — Jul 15 PBOC 6-month buyout reverse repo CNY1.4tn with CNY900bn maturity → net +CNY500bn; 7-day OMO rate held at 1.40% with no LPR-cut signal while oil lifts imported-inflation risk (PBOC/新华网).

chinaChanged.text: PBOC Jul 15 buyout RR: CNY1.4tn (14000亿元) 6-month operation; CNY900bn maturing → net +CNY500bn (5000亿元) (新华网).
Q2 GDP 4.3% y/y (NBS, Jul 15) vs ~4.5% consensus — weakest since Q4 2022; Q2 q/q +0.9%; H1 GDP +4.7% to CNY69.57tn.
A-shares Wed: Shanghai −0.29% to 3,955.58, CSI 300 −0.20% to 4,786.78 — GDP disappointment and semi profit-taking dominated onshore risk appetite.
H-shares caught the global CPI/semi bid: Hang Seng +1.40% to 24,681.10 vs Shanghai −0.29% — sharp A/H divergence (RTHK/华尔街见闻 tape).
China growth/policy desks to watch for follow-through: Caixin and 第一财经 on GDP miss implications and whether quantity easing morphs into price tools.

chinaImplies.text: CNY500bn buyout RR net cushions tax-period funding stress but does not neutralize oil-driven import-bill risk — quantity easing ≠ LPR pivot.
H-shares tracking global CPI/AI relief while A-shares price the GDP miss means onshore beta remains domestic-growth constrained.
If TSMC confirms AI demand and Warsh stays non-committal on near-term hikes, H-tech can extend; weak GDP keeps ChiNext/A-share semis fragile.

chinaDivergences: Kospi +6.24% and Hang Seng +1.40% vs Shanghai −0.29% after a Q2 GDP miss — CPI/AI relief is pricing offshore Asia hardest; onshore A-shares need a growth or policy catalyst, not just global beta.

assetFramework.regime: Fed path via real yields — CPI/PPI-vs-oil tug; AI earnings as secondary support
assetFramework.driver: Jul 14 close S&P +0.38% / Nasdaq +0.90% post-CPI; ASML FY sales raise to €43–45bn
assetFramework.read: Rates-relief + AI-equipment beta held; IBM's prior miss looks idiosyncratic vs ASML/Kospi leadership
assetFramework.invalidator: Equities fall while yields also fall for 2+ sessions — growth fear replacing the rates regime

assetFramework.regime: Inflation prints vs consensus + Fed communication — data-led range
assetFramework.driver: CPI and PPI both missed; Warsh kept hawkish filter without forward guidance
assetFramework.read: Disinflation prints dominated the post-CPI move; Warsh capped how far duration can rally
assetFramework.invalidator: 10y back above 4.65% without a hot data print — term premium / fiscal regime

assetFramework.regime: Rate differentials with a Hormuz safe-haven bid
assetFramework.driver: July hike odds collapsed post-CPI/PPI; Brent still trading ~$85–86
assetFramework.read: Softer Fed path vs oil/geo bid roughly offsets — no decisive DXY break
assetFramework.invalidator: DXY breaks lower while September hike odds hold ~60%

assetFramework.regime: Real-yield beta day-to-day with a CB/fiscal-hedge floor
assetFramework.driver: Post-CPI COMEX settle +1.60% at $4,061.10 (prior session); watching whether oil spoils the real-yield bid
assetFramework.read: Traded classic real-yield beta on the CPI close; oil is the competing impulse
assetFramework.invalidator: Gold falls >3% on a day real yields decline

assetFramework.regime: Geopolitical supply risk — Hormuz premium dominates demand signals
assetFramework.driver: Brent trading ~$85–86 Wed after Jul 14 settle $84.73; Iran blockade / strike risk persists
assetFramework.read: Supply-risk beta intact — overwriting June CPI energy relief for forward inflation
assetFramework.invalidator: Brent settles below $78 with Hormuz crossings >40/day

assetFramework.regime: Domestic growth/liquidity vs selective global risk beta — A/H split
assetFramework.driver: Shanghai −0.29% on Q2 GDP 4.3% miss; Hang Seng +1.40%; PBOC net +CNY500bn buyout RR
assetFramework.read: Liquidity landed but A-shares priced growth disappointment; H-shares caught global CPI/AI bid
assetFramework.invalidator: Shanghai reclaims 4,000 on rising volume with DR007 ≤1.40%

assetFramework.regime: PBOC fix as policy signal; quantity easing without price easing
assetFramework.driver: 7-day rate held 1.40%; buyout RR net +CNY500bn; oil mid-$80s lifts import-inflation risk
assetFramework.read: Stable-fix regime — quantity tools active, LPR pivot not signaled
assetFramework.invalidator: Fix persistently weaker than model estimates for a week

assetFramework.regime: High-beta global liquidity asset in the oil→Fed chain
assetFramework.driver: CPI/PPI cooled July hike odds; Brent still >$85; BlockBeats crypto–macro tape
assetFramework.read: Still priced as liquidity beta — Fed-odds/oil chain dominates crypto policy headlines
assetFramework.invalidator: BTC rallies while Brent >$85 and September hike odds rise

signals.name: Core disinflation confirmed — CPI and PPI both miss
signals.evidence: CPI core flat m/m / 2.6% y/y; PPI final demand −0.3% m/m / 5.5% y/y; July hold odds ~83% post-CPI (BLS; CME FedWatch).
signals.mechanism: Back-to-back downside surprises vs consensus ease near-term re-hike urgency and support duration/semis — unless officials dismiss prints as backward-looking vs oil.
signals.disprovedIf: September hike probability reclaims >70% and 10y yields break above 4.65% within one week.

signals.name: ASML re-accelerates AI equipment demand narrative
signals.evidence: Q2 sales €9.3bn beat; FY26 sales raised to €43–45bn; +30% low-NA EUV capacity plan for 2027 (ASML IR).
signals.mechanism: Primary-source capacity and sales guidance raise reduces 'AI peak' fear and validates Korea/US memory/equipment beta into TSMC.
signals.disprovedIf: TSMC guides down H2 AI demand or ASML cancels the capacity add within a quarter.

signals.name: Hormuz oil premium still overwrites June CPI energy relief
signals.evidence: Brent trading ~$85–86 Wed after Jul 14 settle $84.73; blockade/strike risk remains the supply story.
signals.mechanism: Forward energy inflation risk limits how much June CPI/PPI can reprice the Fed path.
signals.disprovedIf: Brent settles below $78 for three sessions with Hormuz crossings >40/day.

signals.name: Korea/H-shares catch AI+CPI bid; A-shares do not
signals.evidence: Kospi +6.24%, Hang Seng +1.40% vs Shanghai −0.29% after Q2 GDP 4.3% miss (Yonhap; NBS).
signals.mechanism: Offshore Asia prices global rates/AI relief; onshore China prices domestic growth disappointment despite liquidity injection.
signals.disprovedIf: CSI 300 reclaims 4,850 with northbound inflows >CNY5bn for two sessions.

signals.name: Warsh hawkish filter survives soft inflation prints
signals.evidence: Congressional testimony Jul 14–15: commitment to 2% inflation, work remaining, no victory lap on one soft print (Fed/wire coverage).
signals.mechanism: Chair sets the interpretive frame — markets must weigh backward-looking CPI/PPI against forward oil.
signals.disprovedIf: Warsh explicitly downplays September hike risk and cites June disinflation as sufficient progress.

signals.name: TSMC is the falsifier for the ASML/Kospi rebound
signals.evidence: TSMC reports Jul 16 after ASML raise and Kospi +6%; prior Q2 guide US$39.0–40.2bn (TSMC IR).
signals.mechanism: If TSMC confirms AI demand/capex, the semi beta can extend; a soft guide would mark ASML/Kospi as a one-day relief rally.
signals.disprovedIf: TSMC raises H2 outlook with AI demand commentary and Kospi holds 7,200+ through the print.

watchItems.headline: TSMC Q2 results and H2 AI demand guide
watchItems.why: STRONG ASML signal meets the week's main AI-demand falsifier.
watchItems.watch: Jul 16 14:00 Taiwan — revenue vs US$39.0–40.2bn guide; GM; Q3/FY commentary on AI.
watchItems.trigger: Soft guide or AI demand caution that knocks Kospi >3% and SOX lower.
watchItems.invalidator: In-line/beat with constructive H2 AI commentary and Kospi holding 7,200+.

watchItems.headline: Hormuz / Brent settle band
watchItems.why: STRONG energy-premium signal that can overwrite June CPI/PPI.
watchItems.watch: Brent daily settle; CENTCOM / Hormuz flow headlines.
watchItems.trigger: Brent settles above $85 for 3 sessions or crossings stay depressed.
watchItems.invalidator: Brent settle below $80 with crossings >40/day and no new escalation for 5 days.

watchItems.headline: September hike odds after double inflation miss
watchItems.why: Tests whether Warsh + oil prevent a dovish reprice.
watchItems.watch: CME FedWatch September probability; 10y vs post-CPI ~4.57% area (Treasury curve).
watchItems.invalidator: September odds fall below 40% with Brent below $82.

watchItems.headline: A/H divergence after GDP miss
watchItems.why: MODERATE China signal — liquidity vs growth.
watchItems.watch: Shanghai vs Hang Seng; Caixin/Yicai policy follow-through; DR007 vs 1.40%.
watchItems.trigger: Shanghai closes below 3,900 on rising volume despite ample liquidity.
watchItems.invalidator: Shanghai reclaims 4,000 with Hang Seng leadership broadening onshore.

watchItems.headline: Contrarian — CPI/PPI relief fully priced into semis
watchItems.why: Kospi +6% and ASML raise crowd the long-AI trade.
watchItems.watch: Kospi giving back >50% of Wednesday's gain into/after TSMC.
watchItems.trigger: Both Kospi fade and TSMC soft guide = crowded relief trade unwinding.
watchItems.invalidator: Kospi holds gains through TSMC with SOX extending.

sources: Bureau of Labor Statistics (June CPI archive; June PPI), ASML IR (Q2 2026), TSMC IR (Q2 2026 page), NBS (H1/Q2 GDP), 新华网 (PBOC Jul 15 buyout RR), Federal Reserve, US Treasury yield curve, CME FedWatch, Reuters (via Business Times Jul 14 US close), Yonhap (Kospi Jul 15), Yahoo Finance (quote checks), 华尔街见闻, Caixin Global, 第一财经 (Yicai), BlockBeats, TradingEconomics (Brent trading range).
singleSource: Brent ~$85–86 Wednesday trading range (spot/CFD tape via TradingEconomics/FXStreet — not an exchange settle); Jul 15 US cash session was still open at publish time so US levels after Jul 14 close are quote checks only.
